title: Surface Areas and Volumes of 3D Solids
subject: Mathematics
learningOutcomes: Calculate Volume, Curved Surface Area (CSA), and Total Surface Area (TSA) of cylinders, cones, and spheres.
Visualize 3D solid geometries in interactive WebGL.

objectives.text: Calculate the volume of a cylinder with radius 4 and height 8.
Switch to Cone view and adjust slant height.

theory: For Cylinder: $V = \pi r^2 h$, $CSA = 2\pi r h$, $TSA = 2\pi r(r+h)$. For Cone: $V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$, $CSA = \pi r l$. For Sphere: $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$, $SA = 4\pi r^2$.
hints: Rotate the 3D WebGL mesh by clicking and dragging in the viewport.
Use the wireframe toggle button to inspect inner geometric dimensions.

tasks.instruction: Adjust the Radius slider above 5 units.

observationPrompts: If the radius of a cylinder is doubled while keeping height constant, by what factor does its volume increase?

realWorldExamples.title: Water Tank Capacity
realWorldExamples.description: Engineers compute the volume of cylindrical water tanks to determine storage capacity in liters.

commonMistakes: Forgetting that doubling radius quadruples volume ($r^2$), whereas doubling height only doubles volume.
